decks.notes: This deck focuses on utilizing the powerful combo potential of Underworld Breach to consistently generate value and win the game through recursion and card advantage. The deck includes various fast mana sources and powerful spells to quickly assemble the combo and overwhelm the opponent. The key strategy revolves around casting spells from the graveyard with Underworld Breach to generate infinite loops or powerful synergies that ultimately lead to victory. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion further enhances the deck's consistency and ability to execute its game plan effectively.

The deck focuses on assembling a powerful combination of artifacts, specifically Coveted Jewel, to generate mana and card advantage while disrupting the opponent's game plan. It utilizes cards like Tinker, Paradoxical Outcome, and Tezzeret to find and abuse the Jewel, along with support from various mana rocks and control elements like Force of Will. The goal is to quickly establish a dominant board presence and overwhelm the opponent with the sheer power of the Jewel's abilities.

This deck focuses on quickly ramping up mana with artifacts like Mishra's Workshop and Grim Monolith to play powerful threats like Glaring Fleshraker and The One Ring. Tezzeret, Cruel Captain and Karn, the Great Creator provide additional utility with their abilities to search for artifacts and disrupt the opponent's strategy. The inclusion of cards like Trinisphere and Chalice of the Void helps to control the opponent's spells while cards like Scrawling Crawler and Manifold Key synergize well with artifacts in the deck. Mystic Forge and Tolarian Academy provide additional card advantage and mana generation to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive plays.

The deck focuses on using Oath of Druids to cheat powerful creatures into play and control the game with counterspells and removal. The goal is to quickly ramp into playing game-winning threats like Atraxa, Grand Unifier while disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Force of Will and Wasteland. The deck also includes powerful draw spells like Ancestral Recall and Dig Through Time to ensure it has the resources needed to stay ahead. Additionally, the deck includes versatile planeswalkers like Oko, Thief of Crowns and powerful sideboard options like Pyroblast and Veil of Summer to adapt to different matchups.

The strategy of this deck revolves around disruption and card advantage through the use of control cards like Force of Will and Spell Pierce, along with card draw spells like Ancestral Recall and Dig Through Time. The key focus is to control the game through counterspells and removal while generating advantage with cards like Lórien Revealed and Vexing Bauble. Additionally, the inclusion of companion Lurrus of the Dream-Den allows for additional value and recursion. This Dimir Control deck aims to outlast opponents with its efficient removal and card advantage engine.

This Dimir Control deck focuses on using disruption and removal spells to control the opponent's board while generating card advantage through cards like Ancestral Recall and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student. The deck also utilizes counterspells like Force of Will and Mana Drain to protect its game plan and disrupt the opponent's strategy. Lurrus of the Dream-Den acts as a companion to provide additional value by recurring small permanents. The deck aims to outvalue the opponent and grind out a win through control and card advantage.

This Dimir Control deck focuses on using efficient removal spells and counterspells to control the board and disrupt the opponent's game plan. It also includes powerful draw spells like Ancestral Recall and card selection with Brainstorm to maintain card advantage. The deck includes key win conditions in the form of powerful creatures like Psychic Frog and control finishers like Lórien Revealed. By utilizing strong disruption and efficient threats, this deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver opponents in Vintage 1 vs 1 matches.

The deck focuses on utilizing creatures with graveyard recursion and utilizing Survival of the Fittest to quickly assemble a threatening board presence. By discarding cards like Vengevine, Hollow One, and Basking Rootwalla to search for key pieces like Collector Ouphe and Vibrance, the deck can quickly overwhelm opponents with aggressive creatures and resilient threats. The inclusion of cards like Gaea's Cradle and Ignoble Hierarch allow for explosive mana acceleration, while disruptive elements like Chalice of the Void and Detective's Phoenix can disrupt opponents' strategies and protect the deck's game plan.

The deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's strategies while maintaining control of the game through counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage. It aims to keep the opponent off balance with disruptive creatures like Psychic Frog and Orcish Bowmasters, while also utilizing powerful spells like Ancestral Recall and Time Walk to gain an advantage. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion provides additional value by allowing the player to cast cheap threats from the graveyard. The deck is designed to outmaneuver and outlast opponents through resource management and strategic play.

The deck focuses on assembling the Doomsday combo to win the game quickly, using cards like Dark Ritual to generate mana and draw cards, while protecting the combo with counterspells like Force of Will. Lurrus of the Dream-Den serves as a companion that can be cast from outside the game, providing additional value. The goal is to set up a pile of cards with Doomsday that allows the player to win the game on the following turn, typically involving casting Thassa's Oracle to win by emptying the library.

The deck focuses on controlling the opponent's plays through counterspells and removal, while also utilizing card advantage and manipulation through card draw and tutors. It makes use of powerful cards like Ancestral Recall and Force of Will to maintain control of the game, along with disruptive elements like Strip Mine and Wasteland to disrupt the opponent's mana base. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as the companion gives the deck additional value by allowing for recursion of key permanents.

The main concept of this deck is to control the opponent's plays through a combination of counterspells, removal, and disruptive creatures like Lavinia, Azorius Renegade and Orcish Bowmasters. The deck aims to maintain board control and disrupt the opponent's strategy while also having access to powerful spells like Time Walk and Ancestral Recall to gain advantage. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion allows for additional value and recursion of key cards, adding another layer of resilience to the deck.

The key concept of this Dimir Control deck is to disrupt the opponent's strategy through counterspells and removal while generating card advantage with cantrips and draw spells. Additionally, utilizing efficient threats like Orcish Bowmasters and Psychic Frog to apply pressure and control the board. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion provides an additional recursive threat and value engine, allowing the deck to have resilience in longer games. The deck aims to grind out the opponent while maintaining control of the game state.

The deck focuses on controlling the game with a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and disruptive creatures like Orcish Bowmasters and Psychic Frog. It also utilizes powerful cards like Ancestral Recall, Time Walk, and Dig Through Time to gain card advantage and maintain control. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion allows for additional value and recursion of key cards. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while slowly grinding out a win through card advantage and efficient threats.

This deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's strategy with a combination of powerful counterspells like Force of Will and Mana Drain, removal spells like Fatal Push, and disruptive creatures like Psychic Frog. The deck also leverages card advantage with Ancestral Recall, Dig Through Time, and Treasure Cruise, while using Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion to recur key threats or answers from the graveyard. The deck aims to control the game through resource denial with cards like Wasteland and Strip Mine, while maintaining card advantage and tempo to outmaneuver the opponent.

The deck focuses on utilizing artifacts and mana acceleration to quickly assemble the Coveted Jewel combo, which generates an overwhelming advantage in card advantage and mana resources. The deck aims to control the game through disruptive spells like Force of Will and Chalice of the Void, while also utilizing powerful planeswalkers like Tezzeret and Karn to further solidify its board presence. The deck can quickly close out games by using Tinker to fetch key cards like Time Vault or The One Ring, overwhelming opponents with its powerful synergies and efficient win conditions.
